The dense blowing dust created very hazardous driving conditions during the latter portion of the rush hour. Fortunately, no accidents or injuries were reported due to the dust storm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms developed during the afternoon hours across the central deserts, to the southeast of the greater Phoenix metropolitan area. The storms produced strong gusty outflow winds in excess of 40 mph which spread to the northwest and created dust storm conditions. Dense blowing dust overspread northwest Pinal county as well as the greater Phoenix area; many trained weather spotters reported visibilities at or below one quarter mile between 4 pm and 7 pm. A few storms also produced locally heavy rain with peak rain rates in excess of one inch per hour.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms developed to the south and southwest of Casa Grande during the afternoon hours on September 6th. The storms generated strong, gusty ouflow winds in excess of 40 mph which spread to the north and into the greater Phoenix metropolitan area. The winds stirred up significant amounts of dust and dirt, which produced a dust storm. The dust storm spread north across the greater Phoenix area from about 5 pm through 7 pm. At 524 pm, a trained weather spotter 3 miles east of the Mesa Riverview Mall reported visibility below one quarter mile in a dust storm. Another trained spotter 4 miles southwest of Wittmann reported similar conditions in a dust storm at 543 pm. The dense blowing dust created very hazardous driving conditions in the Phoenix area during the latter portion of the rush hour. Fortunately, no serious accidents or injuries were reported as a result of the dust storm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms developed during the afternoon hours across the central deserts, to the southeast of the greater Phoenix metropolitan area. The storms produced strong gusty outflow winds in excess of 40 mph which spread to the northwest and created dust storm conditions. Dense blowing dust overspread northwest Pinal county as well as the greater Phoenix area; many trained weather spotters reported visibilities at or below one quarter mile between 4 pm and 7 pm. A few storms also produced locally heavy rain with peak rain rates in excess of one inch per hour.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Isolated to scattered showers and thunderstorms developed across portions of western Pinal county during the late afternoon hours on September 6th. A few of the storms produced locally heavy rain with peak rain rates in excess of 2 inches per hour. At 502 pm, a trained weather spotter 8 miles southwest of the Ak-Chin Village measured 0.53 inches of rain in a 15 minute period. Although no serious flooding issues were reported with the heavy rain, a Flood Advisory was issued at 445 pm for west central Pinal county, primarily for the area from the town of Maricopa eastward to Sacaton. The heavy rain caused area washes to run, as well as ponding of water on roads and normally dry areas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very moist air of around 1.75 inches of precipitable water contributed to the development of thunderstorms across much of southeast Arizona. Some storms produced very heavy rainfall, primarily in Pima and Pinal Counties.  One storm even produced a brief funnel cloud near Tucson.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rainfall reports between 2 and 4.5 inches caused extensive wash and street flooding in Oro Valley which resulted in numerous road closures. Where washes crossed roadways, damage occurred to roadside ditches and flood control embankments. A motorist was rescued from flood waters at Tangerine and La Canada.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moist southeast flow around high pressure over central Arizona resulted in thunderstorms from Sierra Vista northwest to Ajo.  Some storms produced heavy rain and damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds caused damage in Green Valley.  Several trees were downed along Abrego and La Canada Drives and power outages resulted. Winds peeled a carport and tin roof off a residence in Pueblo Estates. Railroad crossing arms were also damaged by the strong winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant moisture remained over southeast Arizona, leading to the development of widespread thunderstorm activity.  Some storms produced heavy rain, large hail and damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Between 2 and 3 inches of rain fell in a little over an hour in parts of Tucson.  Heavy rain caused the roof of part of the Tucson Mall to collapse, creating water damage in the JC Penney store.  The collapse damaged an air conditioner which caused a water line leak that led to more extensive water damage in the store.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant moisture remained over southeast Arizona, leading to the development of widespread thunderstorm activity.  Some storms produced heavy rain, large hail and damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain of around 2 inches fell in a short amount of time in the northwest section of Willcox during the afternoon and led to flash flooding by early evening. Saguaro, North Fort Grant and Stewart Road were all closed due to flooding and one vehicle became stalled in flood waters. The flood waters caused a fissure to open up on Saguaro Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant moisture remained over southeast Arizona, leading to the development of widespread thunderstorm activity.  Some storms produced heavy rain, large hail and damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding closed several roads from just south of the Tucson International Airport to Green Valley.  Franco Wash flooded Summit Street and Old Vail Connection Road.  Portions of Old Nogales Highway were also closed. In addition, Whitehouse Canyon Road east of Green Valley was flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moist southeast flow around high pressure over central Arizona resulted in thunderstorms from Sierra Vista northwest to Ajo.  Some storms produced heavy rain and damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds damaged eight homes in the Cowlic area. Three homes sustained major roof damage. One of those also had a wall collapse.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moist southeast flow around high pressure over central Arizona resulted in thunderstorms from Sierra Vista northwest to Ajo.  Some storms produced heavy rain and damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds caused minor roof damage to seven homes in Santa Rosa.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moist southeast flow around high pressure over central Arizona resulted in thunderstorms from Sierra Vista northwest to Ajo.  Some storms produced heavy rain and damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A Department of Defense weather sensor recorded a wind gust to 59 mph which confirmed estimated wind gusts near Stoa Pith.  No damage was reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moist southeast flow around high pressure over central Arizona resulted in thunderstorms from Sierra Vista northwest to Ajo.  Some storms produced heavy rain and damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds damaged two homes in Ali Chukson.  One home had major roof and wall damage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moist southeast flow around high pressure over central Arizona resulted in thunderstorms from Sierra Vista northwest to Ajo.  Some storms produced heavy rain and damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flooding in Gunsight and along Gunsight Wash, which washed out Indian Road 3.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms caused heavy rain and flash flooding near Cordes.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter reported 5.47 inches of rain in 14 hours about ten miles southwest of Cordes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm washed out a main road in Crown King.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Alert gages around Crown King reported between 2.84 and 4.52 inches of rain in an 18 to 20 hour time period.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced quarter sized hail two miles south of Pinetop-Lakeside.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Quarter sized hail and some slightly larger stones were reported in Lakeside.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms produced up to three inches of rain between 945 PM MST on July 15 and 1215 AM MST on July 16. This caused flash flooding in Pine Canyon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from thunderstorms caused Pine Creek to overflow Bradshaw Road making it impassible.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms produced up to three inches of rain between 945 PM MST on July 15 and 1215 AM MST on July 16. This caused flash flooding in Pine Canyon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms produced 3 inches of rain in just over three hours in Pine.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist southeast flow of air coupled with an embedded mid level trough resulted in thunderstorms across southeast Arizona from the Tucson Metro eastward. Some storms produced heavy rain, mainly in Cochise County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding was first reported along Davis Road east of Tombstone where 5 vehicles were stuck in flood waters. Another motorist was stuck on Frontier Road. Two homes were also flooded within the city of Tombstone.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist southeast airflow increased precipitable water values to over 1.75 inches. Thunderstorms developed over much of southeast Arizona.  Storms produced heavy rain and flash flooding over Pima and Cochise Counties. Damaging winds occurred in Cochise County and strong outflows in Pima County created a dust storm that tracked northwest across Ironwood National Forest.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rains earlier in the afternoon and persistent moderate rain into the early evening resulted in a tributary of the Brawley Wash flowing over Cocio Road near White Eagle Road.  A motorist was rescued from his vehicle which had been washed downstream.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant moisture remained over southeast Arizona, leading to the development of widespread thunderstorm activity.  Some storms produced heavy rain, large hail and damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain over 2 inches in less than an hour caused extensive urban flash flooding in parts of the Tucson metropolitan area from Drexel Heights to Casas Adobes to Oro Valley. Area fire department responded to nearly a dozen incidents involving vehicles stranded in flood waters and performed several six swift water rescues. A mother and child were rescued from 2 feet of flowing water at River Road and First Avenue. Two vehicles were stuck in flowing water on Camino Del Cerro Road under Interstate 10. Four teenagers were rescued from an SUV on South Oak Tree Drive. Another rescue was performed on two occupants of a stranded vehicle near Ina and Silverbell. Numerous roads were closed due to flood waters into the night.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms causes heavy rain and street flooding in Flagstaff.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm closed the intersection of Dupont and Leroux. Flooding was also reported at Agassiz St and Rt 66. Lightning strikes caused power outages in Downtown Flagstaff.  1/4 inch hail was reported on the NAU Campus.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain fell across the Grand Canyon from the Grand Canyon Village to Supai Village during the overnight hours.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Supai Village Police reported a 3 to 4 foot rise in Havasu Creek near Supai Village. The campground was evacuated. The trail bridges were compromised and the people on the west side of Havasu Creek were stranded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A continued southeast flow of moisture coupled with a mid level trough resulted in thunderstorms over Pima and Pinal Counties. Several storms produced damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds damaged three homes and a cellular tower near Menager's Dam.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A mid level trough approached from the northeast and acted on attendant moisture to produce thunderstorms, flash flooding and damaging winds across eastern and central Pima County.
A mid level trough approached from the northeast and acted on attendant moisture to produce thunderstorms, flash flooding and damaging winds across eastern and central Pima County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds downed a six inch tree branch near Drexel and Houghton Roads.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant moisture of 1.75 inches of precipitable water and weak upper level winds remained across southern Arizona. Thunderstorms developed early in the morning over saturated areas that had received rain the prior afternoon and evening, resulting in flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The second heavy rain of the morning overwhelmed a roof drain system on Continental School in Green Valley. Ceiling tiles collapsed from the weight of the rain and carpets were damaged. In addition, a motorist lost control of a vehicle in heavy rain along Interstate 19 near Green Valley.  The vehicle left the roadway and crashed into a small tree, injuring the driver.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms with heavy rain temporarily closed Highway 160 southwest of Tuba City.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Tuba City Police reported minor flooding from heavy rain in the Kerley Valley.  Water covered Highway160 and caused a temporary road closure.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms in the Bagdag area of far southwest Yavapai County caused heavy rain and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain (2.23 inches) fell over the Bagdad area. This caused flash flooding in the Bagdad Mine area. Portions of Highway 97 where washed out from mile marker 159 to 165.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level low pressure area over southern Nevada acted upon moisture already in place over northern Arizona to produce showers and thunderstorms with heavy rain and strong winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused two feet of water to flow over Navajo Route 15 near the community of Cornfields.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level low pressure area over southern Nevada acted upon moisture already in place over northern Arizona to produce showers and thunderstorms with heavy rain and strong winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Street flooding was reported in St Johns. Manhole covers were being displaced because there was so much water.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms with heavy rain in the Cataract/Havasu Creek drainage during the evening of August 2nd caused flooding problems in the Village of Supai and the Havasupai Campground.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flood waters from heavy rain that fell on Tuesday evening (July 31) reached Supai Village between 230 and 300 AM. Water up to several feet caused trails and bridges to become impassible. This stranded several hikers and flooded area campgrounds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms with heavy rain caused a rock fall north that injured a hiker on the north side of the Colorado River.  The heavy rain cause damage to the River Trail on the south side of the Colorado River.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from thunderstorms caused damage to the River Trail on the south side of the Colorado River about a mile from Phantom Ranch.  The heavy rain also caused a rock fall that injured a 19 year old hiker in a narrow canyon about 5 miles north of Phantom Ranch.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level low pressure area over southern Nevada acted upon moisture already in place over northern Arizona to produce showers and thunderstorms with heavy rain and strong winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A peak wind gust of 70 MPH was recorded at the St Johns Airport.  Law enforcement also reported that trees were blown down in town.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant moisture trapped under the monsoon high resulted in widespread thunderstorms over all but far eastern sections of southeast Arizona. Storms produced very heavy rain, flash flooding and damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Very heavy rain that began the previous afternoon and continued through the night, totaled 3 to 5 inches upstream near the Quijotoa Mountains.  Runoff in Santa Rosa Wash damaged a berm along Route 15 causing water to flow across and close this road near North Komelik.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant moisture remained over southern Arizona. Thunderstorms were most predominant southeast of a line from Safford to Sells, but were absent across much of the Tucson metropolitan area.  Thunderstorms produced heavy rain and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain east of Sierra Vista caused flash flooding of washes that cross Moson Road resulting in closure of the road from the Charleston Road intersection south to near Hereford Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant moisture remained over southern Arizona. Thunderstorms were most predominant southeast of a line from Safford to Sells, but were absent across much of the Tucson metropolitan area.  Thunderstorms produced heavy rain and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain produced flash flooding in Saint David. One bridge on a county road was washed out and flood waters entered one home, damaging the laundry room.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level low pressure area over southern Nevada acted upon moisture already in place over northern Arizona to produce showers and thunderstorms with heavy rain and strong winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Sunset Wash overflowed on to Sunset Drive. Police set up barricades to close the road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another surge of monsoon moisture led to an outbreak of thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. A low pressure system further enhanced the storms on the 22nd.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several roads on the way to the Bar Ten Ranch were washed out.  In addition, a wall of water, mud, boulders and trees washed out of the mouth of Fern Glen Canyon at Mile 168 of the Colorado River.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced heavy rain and flash flooding in Havasu Creek near Supai.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Havasupai Tribal Police reported a four foot rise in Havasu Creek. Bridges over the creek washed out.  River runners on the Colorado River recorded a flash flood in National Canyon that dumped rocks, trees, mud, and 2 foot deep foam into the Colorado River.  This enhanced the rapid at the mouth of National Canyon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A mid level trough acted on ample moisture to produce thunderstorms capable of producing damaging winds across Santa Cruz and western Pima Counties.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The AWOS at Pinal Air Park (KMZJ) recorded a gust to 67 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from thunderstorms caused minor flooding in Sedona and rock slides in Oak Creek Canyon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Water was flowing across Highway 89A in West Sedona. Sunset Drive and Shelby Drive were close to do water on the roadway. Between 1.00 and 1.50 inches of rain was recorded in Sedona.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from thunderstorms caused minor flooding in Sedona and rock slides in Oak Creek Canyon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Very heavy rain fell along Oak Creek Canyon. Debris fell onto the road at the upper end of the canyon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: As the monsoon high shifted northward, moisture increased across all of southeast Arizona, resulting in widespread thunderstorms.  Some of these thunderstorms produced damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Spotters in Hereford measured thunderstorm wind gusts to 62 mph and reported shingles blown off the roof of one house.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A continued southeast flow of moisture coupled with a mid level trough resulted in thunderstorms over Pima and Pinal Counties. Several storms produced damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds downed a tree at Mission Manor Park in Tucson and flipped over a loosely anchored small metal shed at San Joaquin Road and West Milky Way Drive.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant moisture remained in place. The monsoon high was nearly overhead which resulted in isolated but very slow moving thunderstorms that produced heavy rain and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused most washes that cross Indian Road 19 to flow rapidly.  Motorists were rescued from their vehicle near South Komelik.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant moisture trapped under the monsoon high resulted in widespread thunderstorms over all but far eastern sections of southeast Arizona. Storms produced very heavy rain, flash flooding and damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms winds toppled trees near Grant and Oracle Roads. One tree fell on top of a car.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: With the monsoon high centered over Colorado, moist southeast airflow over Arizona resulted in thunderstorms, mainly near the international border. Some storms produced damaging wind gusts.

Abundant moisture of 1.75 inches of precipitable water and weak upper level winds remained across southern Arizona. Thunderstorms developed early in the morning over saturated areas that had received rain the prior afternoon and evening, resulting in flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: During the morning hours, brief but heavy rain fell on saturated ground, causing washes to quickly fill and flow across roads. Portions of Camino Del Sol and Mission Roads were closed due to flooding in and near Green Valley.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant moisture trapped under the monsoon high resulted in widespread thunderstorms over all but far eastern sections of southeast Arizona. Storms produced very heavy rain, flash flooding and damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two to four inches of heavy rain caused flash flooding of streets and homes in Covered Wells.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant moisture trapped under the monsoon high resulted in widespread thunderstorms over all but far eastern sections of southeast Arizona. Storms produced very heavy rain, flash flooding and damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flash flooding in Santa Rosa where at least two homes were flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Enough rainfall occurred during August to ease drought conditions slightly in Pinal County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Extreme drought (D3) continued in portions of Pinal County in August but enough rainfall occurred for conditions to improve to the severe drought or (D2) category during the latter half of the month. Since the impacts of this drought began with the winter of 2010-2011, most have already been mitigated. Rainfall amounts generally totaled between 0.75 inch and 1.5 inches. Despite the rain, dry antecedent conditions contributed to several dust storm events as thunderstorm outflow winds kicked up dust, which ultimately developed into haboobs. These large areas of blowing dust rolled northward across portions of Pinal County on at least three occasions in August.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Enough rainfall occurred during August to ease drought conditions slightly in Pinal County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Extreme drought (D3) continued in portions of Pinal County in August but enough rainfall occurred for conditions to improve to the severe drought or (D2) category during the latter half of the month. Since the impacts of this drought began with the winter of 2010-2011, most have already been mitigated. Rainfall amounts generally totaled between 0.75 inch and 1.5 inches. Despite the rain, dry antecedent conditions contributed to several dust storm events as thunderstorm outflow winds kicked up dust, which ultimately developed into haboobs. These large areas of blowing dust rolled northward across portions of Pinal County on at least three occasions in August.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: As the monsoon high shifted northward, moisture increased across all of southeast Arizona, resulting in widespread thunderstorms.  Some of these thunderstorms produced damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds damaged several trees in Oro Valley, Marana and Casas Adobes on the northwest side of the Tucson metro area. A spotter near Oracle Junction measured a wind gust of 60 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms developed across portions of southern Gila county during the afternoon hours on August 19th, and they produced locally heavy rain, especially to the south and east of Globe. Rainfall rates were in excess of 2 inches per hour with the stronger thunderstorms. Flash flooding developed near the town of San Carlos. Fortunately, no injuries were reported due to the flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms developed across southern Gila county during the afternoon hours on August 19th, and they produced locally heavy rain with rain rates in excess of 2 inches per hour. Some of the heaviest rain occurred southeast of Globe, along Indian Highway 6 between Apache Gold Casino and San Carlos. At 540 pm, San Carlos Tribal Police reported flooding along the Gilson wash as well as nearby areas on Indian Highway 6. At 6 pm, the San Carlos Tribal Police reported a motorist vehicle stranded in the wash along Highway 6, or the old San Carlos Road. The flooding led to the issuance of a Flash Flood Warning at 452 pm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Enough rainfall occurred during August to ease drought conditions slightly in Pinal County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Extreme drought (D3) continued in portions of Pinal County in August but enough rainfall occurred for conditions to improve to the severe drought or (D2) category during the latter half of the month. Since the impacts of this drought began with the winter of 2010-2011, most have already been mitigated. Rainfall amounts generally totaled between 0.75 inch and 1.5 inches. Despite the rain, dry antecedent conditions contributed to several dust storm events as thunderstorm outflow winds kicked up dust, which ultimately developed into haboobs. These large areas of blowing dust rolled northward across portions of Pinal County on at least three occasions in August.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms developed across the northern portion of the greater Phoenix metropolitan area during the evening hours on August 16th. The storms produced locally heavy rainfall with rain rates well in excess of one inch per hour. The heavy rain led to episodes of flash flooding, which affected the towns of Surprise and Circle City. The flooding led to water across area roads, which promted a number of swift water rescues in the west valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms developed across northern portions of Maricopa county during the evening hours on August 16th, and they produced locally heavy rain with rain rates in excess of one inch per hour. Radar and rain gage data indicated some locations picked up between one half and one and a half inches of rain, especially areas to the north of Surprise. The heavy rain led to excessive urban flooding, which transitioned into flash flooding. A Flash Flood Warning was not issued, however an Urban and Small Stream Flood Advisory was in effect as of 9 pm for portions of north central Maricopa county. As seen on a channel 15 television interview, significant street flooding occurred in portions of Surprise with water at least one foot deep in places. Ponded water approached several homes and actually flowed into the first floor of at least one home, causing significant damage. According to local law enforcement, multiple cars were washed off the road near Dale Lane and 170th Avenue. Rain falling north of the area caused washes to flow rapidly, and water from a flooding wash flowed over the road, trapping a number of drivers. Surprise fire Captain Kevin Spirlong indicated that there were multiple swift water rescues; rescue units had to pluck people from 8 vehicles. It took about 30 firefighters from 6 local agencies to get the residents from the wash which was located near 173rd Avenue and Dixieleta Road. The swift water rescues took place from around 10 pm to midnight. Surprise police spokesman Mark Ortega indicated that there was damage to some of the roads as a result of the flooding and the roads were going to be tested for safety. Fortunately, no injuries were reported as a result of the flash flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A continued southeast flow of moisture coupled with a mid level trough resulted in thunderstorms over Pima and Pinal Counties. Several storms produced damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds downed a tree at Catalina Regional Park.  The tree fell on a park shelter, damaging the roof.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large convective complex formed over south central Arizona during the early morning hours on August 23rd, a result of a weather disturbance acting on a very humid and unstable airmass. The complex of thunderstorms produced locally heavy rain across much of south central Arizona, including the greater Phoenix metropolitan area, beginning shortly after midnight and persisting through sunrise. Peak rainfall rates were between 3 and 4 inches per hour, and the heavy rain led to episodes of flooding and flash flooding, affecting areas from Witmann and New River south through the central portions of Phoenix. Several Urban and Small Stream Flood Advisories were issued for the event, but no Flash Flood Warnings were issued, despite the fact that a Flash Flood Watch remained in effect through 5 am.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A large convective complex developed across the greater Phoenix metropolitan area during the early morning hours on August 23rd. The combination of a weather disturbance and a very humid and unstable airmass allowed the thunderstorms to produce copious amounts of rainfall, with peak rain rates well in excess of 2 inches per hour. Heavy rain began around 130 AM and persisted into the early morning. At 255 AM, a trained weather spotter about 2 miles northeast of Phoenix Sky Harbor Airport reported that he had measured 2 inches of rain since 2 AM. The excessive rain led to significant urban flooding as well as flash flooding; water was reported to be flowing into at least one home in the area. Although no Flash Flood Warnings were in effect at the time, a Flash Flood Watch was in effect through 5 AM, and Urban and Small Stream Flood Advisories were also in effect for central Phoenix. No injuries were reported as a result of the flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Isolated thunderstorms developed across the south central Arizona deserts during the evening hours on September 12th, and some of them affected the greater Phoenix metropolitan area. Some of the thunderstorms developed strong, gusty and damaging outflow winds estimated to be in excess of 50 mph. The winds were sufficiently strong to down a number of trees in the northern portion of the Phoenix area; the diameter of the largest trees approached 12 inches.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Isolated thunderstorms developed across the northern portion of the greater Phoenix metropolitan area, including North Mountain Park, during the evening hours on September 12th. The storms generated strong, damaging outflow winds with peak gusts estimated to be near 65 knots. According to a National Weather Service employee, at 645 pm strong thunderstorm wind gusts downed a number of trees along Bell Road near 7th Street and 7th Avenue. Snapped tree trunks and branches were noted as well, and the diameter of the largest trees downed was estimated to be 12 inches. Additionally, the employee reported street lights not in operation along Bell Road. A reporter from Channel 3 submitted a few storm damage photos which depicted trees that had fallen on top of automobiles, causing moderate damage. One large tree, with a diameter at least 12 inches, fell on top of a block wall, crushing the wall on impact.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A mid level trough acted on ample moisture to produce thunderstorms capable of producing damaging winds across Santa Cruz and western Pima Counties.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds caused roof damage to one residence and damaged several sheds at Diamond Bell Ranch.  An eight inch brick wall was blown over on to a car.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A slow moving cluster of intense thunderstorms developed across far southwestern Arizona during the afternoon hours on September 9th, and they affected the city of Yuma as well as surrounding communities such as Somerton and Fortuna Foothills. Due to the very humid and unstable nature of the atmosphere, the storms generated strong, gusty and damaging microburst winds well in excess of 60 mph. The gusty winds were sufficient to down power lines and tree limbs. Three separate Severe Thunderstorm Warnings were issued for the area around Yuma, due both to the slow movement and continued regeneration of the severe thunderstorms. No injuries were reported due to the severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A cluster of slow moving and very intense thunderstorms developed in the Yuma and Somerton area during the afternoon hours on September 9th. The storms generated strong, gusty and damaging microburst winds. According to a trained weather spotter 4 miles southeast of Somerton, wind gusts estimated to be at least 60 mph picked up and blew a metal shed a distance of approximately 30 yards. Another homeowner in Somerton had a 8 by 10 foot shed shifted several degrees off of its foundation by high winds. Also, the winds broke off a number of tree limbs estimated to be 4 inches or greater in diameter. Finally, as seen in a video posted on ABC15.com, strong thunderstorm wind gusts blew down about 40 feet of block wall in the back yard of an area homeowner. The falling wall nearly fell on top of man who was in the back yard, attempting to see if the 60 mph winds would support him if he fell face-first into the wind.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Around 2 inches of precipitable water was acted upon by a mid level trough to produce thunderstorms which dropped extremely heavy rain in southeast Arizona. Flash flooding was the result.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flash flooding along Interstate 10 west of Mescal and ponding of water three feet deep in the Marsh Station Road underpass. One person was injured in a crash on the interstate after the driver lost control of the vehicle in the flood waters.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Around 2 inches of precipitable water was acted upon by a mid level trough to produce thunderstorms which dropped extremely heavy rain in southeast Arizona. Flash flooding was the result.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused Hickiwan Wash to overflow its banks. Two homes near the wash were flooded and several roads were closed.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered to numerous showers and thunderstorms developed across the south central Arizona deserts, including the greater Phoenix metropolitan area, during the morning hours on September 7th. Some of the thunderstorms produced excessive rainfall, with peak rain rates in excess of 2 inches per hour. The rain led to both urban flooding and flash flooding over portions of the greater Phoenix area. Washes quickly started to flow heavily, and water ponded across roadways and parking lots, stranding motorists.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Scattered to numerous showers and thunderstorms developed across the greater Phoenix area during the morning hours on September 7th, and they continued into the afternoon hours over portions of the west valley. Peak rainfall rates at the height of the morning thunderstorms exceeded 2 inches per hour. The heavy rain caused some of the washes in the west valley to fill quickly and to run heavily during the afternoon hours. According to an on-line article and taped interview on ABC15.com, a Century Link employee became stuck on top of his vehicle in the Waterman wash near the intersection of 155th Avenue and Riggs Road in Goodyear. The man drove his truck into the muddy wash in the early afternoon and became stuck in the mud. Shortly afterwards, flash flooding occurred in the wash  and the vehicle became submerged nearly to its roof. He was trapped on the roof for 2 to 3 hours before being rescued. Goodyear firefighters used an inflatable raft to reach the man, and the rescue was considered to be very dangerous due to the swiftly flowing water. The man was not injured and he told paramedics that he did not need to go to a hospital.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A slow moving cluster of intense thunderstorms developed across far southwestern Arizona during the afternoon hours on September 9th, and they affected the city of Yuma as well as surrounding communities such as Somerton and Fortuna Foothills. Due to the very humid and unstable nature of the atmosphere, the storms generated strong, gusty and damaging microburst winds well in excess of 60 mph. The gusty winds were sufficient to down power lines and tree limbs. Three separate Severe Thunderstorm Warnings were issued for the area around Yuma, due both to the slow movement and continued regeneration of the severe thunderstorms. No injuries were reported due to the severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A cluster of slow moving and intense thunderstorms developed in the Yuma and Somerton areas during the late afternoon hours on September 9th. In addition to damaging microburst winds, the storms produced locally heavy rainfall with peak rain rates well in excess of one inch per hour. At 613 pm, a trained weather spotter 4 miles southeast of Somerton reported very heavy rain. The spotter measured three quarters of an inch of rain in a forty minute period. The heavy rain prompted the issuance of a Small Stream Flood Advisory at 510 pm, and led to flooding of area roads and washes, such as the Fortuna Wash.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant moisture trapped under the monsoon high resulted in widespread thunderstorms over all but far eastern sections of southeast Arizona. Storms produced very heavy rain, flash flooding and damaging wind gusts.
Around 2 inches of precipitable water was acted upon by a mid level trough to produce thunderstorms which dropped extremely heavy rain in southeast Arizona. Flash flooding was the result.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms produced between 2 and 3 inches of rain in less than one hour in Nogales. Rainfall totals reached over 4 inches in some locations. The result was severe urban flooding with water as deep as three feet in the streets of Nogales. Several motorists were rescued from the flood waters in the city and at least one home was flooded with a foot of water. The Nogales Wash briefly topped its banks, covering the railroad tracks which halted all rail traffic into Mexico. Flood waters caused a 20 foot retaining wall to collapse. Debris flew threw a window, crushing an air conditioning unit and allowing water and mud inside the home. Flood waters also damaged an underground telephone cable leaving about 200 customers without telephone service.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Around 2 inches of precipitable water was acted upon by a mid level trough to produce thunderstorms which dropped extremely heavy rain in southeast Arizona. Flash flooding was the result.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain of nearly 3 inches fell on parts of the Chiricahua National Monument, much of which was affected by the Horseshoe 2 Wildfire in 2011. Flash flooding and a 2 to 3 foot debris flow damaged trails and the Bonita Canyon Campground. Bonita Canyon Drive was also covered by the debris flow. The area was subsequently evacuated.  The campground remain closed until August 30th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A mid level trough approached from the northeast and acted on attendant moisture to produce thunderstorms, flash flooding and damaging winds across eastern and central Pima County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from numerous thunderstorms caused several roads to be closed due to flash flooding across the Tucson metropolitan area, including Snyder Road near the Ventana Wash, Redington Road, West Valencia Road  and the Stone Avenue underpass. Three vehicles became stranded in flood waters near Pima and Country Club Roads.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure stretched from the panhandle of Texas into Chihuahua, allowing southwest flow to bring moisture of nearly 1.5 inches of precipitable water into south central Arizona. Thunderstorms developed from Tucson westward.  Some produced damaging winds and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds downed a tree on the median along Campbell Avenue near 6th Street.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure stretched from the panhandle of Texas into Chihuahua, allowing southwest flow to bring moisture of nearly 1.5 inches of precipitable water into south central Arizona. Thunderstorms developed from Tucson westward.  Some produced damaging winds and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flash flooding near Topawa. Two people were rescued from a vehicle that was caught in flood waters along Indian Road 19.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure stretched from the panhandle of Texas into Chihuahua, allowing southwest flow to bring moisture of nearly 1.5 inches of precipitable water into south central Arizona. Thunderstorms developed from Tucson westward.  Some produced damaging winds and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds downed a tree near Skyline Drive and Pontatoc Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure stretched from the panhandle of Texas into Chihuahua, allowing southwest flow to bring moisture of nearly 1.5 inches of precipitable water into south central Arizona. Thunderstorms developed from Tucson westward.  Some produced damaging winds and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds downed a tree which fell on to power lines near Grant Road and Columbus Blvd.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak upper level low in weak southwest flow aloft acted on over 1.5 inches of precipitable water to produce thunderstorms with heavy rain over southern Arizona. Some of the heavy rain resulted in flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flash flooding on the southwest side of Tucson.  A small wash that crosses Valencia Road overflowed, causing closure of the road between Viviana and Camino Verde. Several yards in the area were also flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak upper level disturbance combined with abundant moisture approaching 2 inches of precipitable water to generate widespread thunderstorms across southeast Arizona. Thunderstorms produced flash flooding and some produced damaging winds.  The heavy rain caused flooding of the Brawley Wash on the west side of Tucson during the predawn hours of September 7th. One fatality and one injury resulted.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Very heavy rain caused extensive street flooding in Ajo. Cedar Road was washed out by rapidly flowing flood waters 2 to 3 feet deep. a community health center and a school were also flooded.  Farther south, Ajo Mountain Drive in Organ Pipe National Monument was closed as well as part of nearby Highway 85.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak upper level disturbance combined with abundant moisture approaching 2 inches of precipitable water to generate widespread thunderstorms across southeast Arizona. Thunderstorms produced flash flooding and some produced damaging winds.  The heavy rain caused flooding of the Brawley Wash on the west side of Tucson during the predawn hours of September 7th. One fatality and one injury resulted.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Very heavy rain collapsed ceiling tiles in an Ajo business, damaging some of the goods inside.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak upper level disturbance combined with abundant moisture approaching 2 inches of precipitable water to generate widespread thunderstorms across southeast Arizona. Thunderstorms produced flash flooding and some produced damaging winds.  The heavy rain caused flooding of the Brawley Wash on the west side of Tucson during the predawn hours of September 7th. One fatality and one injury resulted.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding caused the Sahuarita Road to become impassable near Ocotillo View.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak upper level disturbance combined with abundant moisture approaching 2 inches of precipitable water to generate widespread thunderstorms across southeast Arizona. Thunderstorms produced flash flooding and some produced damaging winds.  The heavy rain caused flooding of the Brawley Wash on the west side of Tucson during the predawn hours of September 7th. One fatality and one injury resulted.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds downed a tree near Ajo and Camino Verde Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak upper level disturbance combined with abundant moisture approaching 2 inches of precipitable water to generate widespread thunderstorms across southeast Arizona. Thunderstorms produced flash flooding and some produced damaging winds.  The heavy rain caused flooding of the Brawley Wash on the west side of Tucson during the predawn hours of September 7th. One fatality and one injury resulted.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A vehicle became stranded in flood waters on Arivaca Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak upper level disturbance combined with abundant moisture approaching 2 inches of precipitable water to generate widespread thunderstorms across southeast Arizona. Thunderstorms produced flash flooding and some produced damaging winds.  The heavy rain caused flooding of the Brawley Wash on the west side of Tucson during the predawn hours of September 7th. One fatality and one injury resulted.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms produced 2 to 3 inches of rain in about 90 minutes causing many roads to be inundated by rapidly flowing flood waters on the southwest side of Tucson.  AT least ten vehicles were caught up in the flood waters. Two people were rescued from vehicles at Sandario Road and Kinney Road while three more were rescued from a tree after their vehicle was washed away.  Another five motorists in five separate vehicles were stranded near Ajo Way and Kinney Rpad.  Bopp Road northwest of Ajo Way was washed out which caused Banks Elementary School students to become stranded in school until a detour around the washed out road could be fashioned.  In addition, Gates Pass Road at Via Roma was flooded in the Tucson Mountains. No injuries occurred to any of the motorists.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak upper level disturbance combined with abundant moisture approaching 2 inches of precipitable water to generate widespread thunderstorms across southeast Arizona. Thunderstorms produced flash flooding and some produced damaging winds.  The heavy rain caused flooding of the Brawley Wash on the west side of Tucson during the predawn hours of September 7th. One fatality and one injury resulted.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds blew down an open ended Quonset hut outbuilding and a trailer in Hunter Estates near Duncan. Another trailer sustained considerable roof damage.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN NAVAJO COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A deep and cold winter storm system appoaching Arizona brought strong winds and blowing dust to areas northeast of major terrain features such as the Mogollon Rim and San Fransisco Peaks.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The ASOS at the Winslow Airport reported a peak wind gust of 62 MPH.  The winds gusted over 58 MPH for over an hour.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak upper level disturbance combined with abundant moisture approaching 2 inches of precipitable water to generate widespread thunderstorms across southeast Arizona. Thunderstorms produced flash flooding and some produced damaging winds.  The heavy rain caused flooding of the Brawley Wash on the west side of Tucson during the predawn hours of September 7th. One fatality and one injury resulted.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Up to three inches of rain fell in Duncan, causing flash flooding of most streets in Duncan's Hunter Estates where one home was also flooded. A wash that parallels and but remains just northwest of Carlisle Road climbed to a depth of about 8 feet, flooded Fairgrounds Road. Trees and other debris in the flood waters caused minor surface damage to the bridge at Fairgrounds Road before the waters entered the Gila River.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak upper level disturbance combined with abundant moisture approaching 2 inches of precipitable water to generate widespread thunderstorms across southeast Arizona. Thunderstorms produced flash flooding and some produced damaging winds.  The heavy rain caused flooding of the Brawley Wash on the west side of Tucson during the predawn hours of September 7th. One fatality and one injury resulted.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain of two to four inches occurred the previous afternoon on Tucson's west side near Ajo Way and Kinney Road and directly over the Brawley Wash just west of Sandario Road. In addition, two to three inches of rain fell in the Sierrita Mountains west of Green Valley.  By the time the runoff from the Sierrita Mountains reached the west side of Tucson many hours later, Brawley Wash overflowed its banks. During the overnight hours, five vehicles drove into the flood waters and became stranded along Manville Road just west of Sandario Road. Around 2 am, one man escaped his vehicle before it was washed downstream only to be swept several yards away himself.  He suffered a leg injury. His vehicle was found 200 yards downstream, upside down. The man's wife was found in the vehicle, drown.  Rain redeveloped around daybreak allowing flood conditions to persist through mid morning.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN COCONINO COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A deep and cold winter storm system appoaching Arizona brought strong winds and blowing dust to areas northeast of major terrain features such as the Mogollon Rim and San Fransisco Peaks.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Arizona Department of Transportation anemometer at Two Guns reported a peak wind gust of 70 MPH.  A wind sensor on the edge of Meteor Crater reported sustained winds of 60-70 MPH with a gust to 111 MPH. This location is not representative of the conditions away from the crater rim.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms with locally heavy rainfall developed over portions of northeast Maricopa county during the late evening hours on August 16th, and some of the storms affected the Sunflower Burn area just north of the town of Sunflower. Radar indicated that more than one and one half inches of rain fell in the Burn area since 1030 pm, and the heavy rain led to flash flooding in Sunflower and along Sycamore Creek downstream of the burn. The flooding continued into the early morning hours on August 17th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms developed across northeast Maricopa county, including the area around the Sunflower Burn, during the late evening hours on August 16th. The storms produced locally heavy rainfall over the burn area, and radar indicated that over one and one half inches fell since around 10 pm. Due to the burn conditions, the heavy rain led to flash flooding which affected both the town of Sunflower and Sycamore creek downstream of the burn. Stream gage sensors near the Tonto National Forest Work Center indicated that flow in Sycamore Creek reached 7900 cfs, and the depth of the water was over 10 feet. Flash Flood Warnings were issued for both the area around and downstream of the burn area, starting at 1110 pm and continuing through 245 am early on the 17th. The flash flood warning was converted to a flood warning, which continued through 645 am. Local law enforcement indicated that flooding along Sycamore creek caused forest roads 403 and 414 to be closed. During the peak of flooding, Sycamore creek left its banks and was nearly 40 yards wide in some locations. Rapid debris flow was noted along highway 87 at Round Valley.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough triggered evening thunderstorms over Cochise County that developed westward across southeast Arizona, resulting in pockets of damaging winds and heavy rain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck and destroyed a generator that provided power for Huachuca City Police Department telephone lines.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms developed across the south central deserts during the evening hours on August 18th. In addition to locally heavy rain, the storms produced damaging microburst winds which blew down power poles in the town of Coolidge.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms developed across the south central Arizona deserts during the evening hours on August 18th. Some of the storms generated strong and damaging microburst winds with gusts estimated to be at least 50 knots. According to the Florence Sheriff's office, at 715 pm several power poles were blown down in the town of Coolidge, and a number of power lines were downed as well. Locally heavy rain accompanied the storms over the central deserts, a National Weather Service employee in Maricopa reported 1.26 of rain, and some flooding was reported in the San Tan Valley.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very strong thunderstorms over the Canyon Fire area caused flash flooding in Jacks Canyon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A climber in Jacks Canyon downstream from the Canyon Fire area reported heavy rain and hail started around 1215. He also stated: The primary stage of the flood was from 1245-1430. Secondary stage from 1430-1630 and tertiary 1630- until we exited around 1845. The next morning the water was still flowing but had decreased by 80% or so. During the storm we were having lightning strikes with in a mile every 90secs between 1230-1430. Hail and high winds for 15-30 minutes around 1300. Hail stones around 4-8mm.||I would guess that the size and energy of the flood had to do more with the fact that it was a recent fire area. The storm did move from the East to the South West which pretty much follows the topography of the canyon. The debris was mostly fire damage trees and soil.||No other reports of flooding in that area were received.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Isolated strong thunderstorms developed across portions of the greater Phoenix area during the mid afternoon hours on July 31st. One storm produced a wind gust to 64 mph at the Deer Valley airport. The thunderstorms moved slowly north, then congealed and intensified over the community of Anthem. Very heavy rains, with rainfall rates in excess of 3 inches per hour, were observed and they led to episodes of flash flooding in the community of Anthem. One weather observer reported rainfall in excess of 5 inches in his rain gage. Flooding also occurred in the Deadman Wash near Interstate 17 to the west of Anthem.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Isolated strong thunderstorms developed across the northern portions of the greater  Phoenix area during the afternoon hours on July 31st. During the late afternoon the storms congealed and intensified in the Anthem area, and they produced excessive rainfall which led to episodes of flash flooding. Radar indicated that peak rainfall rates were in excess of 3 inches per hour, and one weather observer measured more than 5 inches of rain within 90 minutes. According to the Anthem Fire Department and Rescue, the heavy rain filled up the Deadman Wash drainages in town and overflowed, leading to severe flooding in a number of homes. Much of the flooding occurred between 6 pm and 7 pm. At about 610 pm flood waters backing up in the wash overtopped a brick wall behind a home causing the wall to collapse. Flood waters then poured into the home, located at 40715 North Erickson Lane, causing severe flood damage. As the wall collapsed, the flood waters slammed into the homeonwner, carrying her across the yard and causing injuries to her.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms were nudged slightly eastward by low pressure to the west of Arizona. Storms produced damaging winds and heavy rain from Tucson eastward.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain over and adjacent to the Monument Fire burn scar caused flash flooding in the Sierra Vista and Hereford areas. Numerous roads were covered with flood waters, mud, boulders and other debris. A vehicle was stuck in the flooded intersection of Bloomfield Road and Kings Ranch Road.  Two people were caught up in flood waters at the Carr Canyon Campsite. A 22 year old male managed to escape up the bank of the wash but a 16 year old female was swept downstream and not located until 4 hours later. Both suffered minor injuries. An elderly couple were rescued from the roof of their vehicle stuck in flood waters at the intersection of Ramsey Road and Burrow Drive.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms developed across the south central Arizona deserts during the afternoon hours on July 14th. Due to the very moist and unstable nature of the atmosphere, the storms tended to produce locally heavy rainfall with rainfall rates well in excess of one inch per hour. The heavy rain led to some flash flooding in the Wickenburg area late in the afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms developed across south central Arizona during the afternoon hours on Saturday July 14th, and they produced locally heavy rainfall which led to episodes of flash flooding in the Wickenburg area. A Flash Flood Warning was issued for Wickenburg at about 5 pm in the afternoon. According to the Maricopa County Sheriff's Department, a 65 year old man died as he attempted to drive across the Hassayampa wash/river near Wickenburg. As he attempted to drive through the wash, the swiftly flowing water carried his vehicle about one quarter of a mile downstream and into some brush, causing him to drown. No other details were made available. In addition to the fatality, crews were dispatched to four water rescues during the evening hours on Saturday according to Kevin Pool of the Wickenburg Fire Department.  One such rescue occurred north of town along US highway 93 in a neighborhood near a wash. In that incident the person was rescued from the top of his vehicle by sheriff's helicopter and taken to a hospital for observation.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture began to return from the southeast, fueling strong thunderstorms over the eastern Mojave Desert. Several storms produced severe weather and/or flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Small amounts of mud washed onto Hualapai Mountain Road between the 5500 and 6000 foot levels.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very moist air of around 1.75 inches of precipitable water contributed to the development of thunderstorms across much of southeast Arizona. Some storms produced very heavy rainfall, primarily in Pima and Pinal Counties.  One storm even produced a brief funnel cloud near Tucson.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several roads, including Manville Road, Pump Station Road and El Tiro Road were flooded on the far west side of Tucson.  Blanco Wash also flooded, stranding a teenager who was horseback riding in the area Derringer Road and Lambert Lane.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist southeast airflow increased precipitable water values to over 1.75 inches. Thunderstorms developed over much of southeast Arizona.  Storms produced heavy rain and flash flooding over Pima and Cochise Counties. Damaging winds occurred in Cochise County and strong outflows in Pima County created a dust storm that tracked northwest across Ironwood National Forest.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding closed Desert Ridge Drive in Green Valley as well as Mission Road near Twin Buttes Cemetery.  Farther north, a car became stuck in flood waters near Ajo Highway and Sandario Road. In Avra Valley and Picture Rocks, Manville Road was closed between Sanders and Trico Roads due to flooding as well as Twin Peaks Road between Sandario and Sanders Roads.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist southeast airflow increased precipitable water values to over 1.75 inches. Thunderstorms developed over much of southeast Arizona.  Storms produced heavy rain and flash flooding over Pima and Cochise Counties. Damaging winds occurred in Cochise County and strong outflows in Pima County created a dust storm that tracked northwest across Ironwood National Forest.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain over the Monument Fire burn scar resulted in flash flooding of canyons and the closure of several roads including Ramsey Road, Coronado Memorial Road and Stone Ridge Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist southeast airflow increased precipitable water values to over 1.75 inches. Thunderstorms developed over much of southeast Arizona.  Storms produced heavy rain and flash flooding over Pima and Cochise Counties. Damaging winds occurred in Cochise County and strong outflows in Pima County created a dust storm that tracked northwest across Ironwood National Forest.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds downed a tree at Fox Hollow Cabins.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very moist air of around 1.75 inches of precipitable water contributed to the development of thunderstorms across much of southeast Arizona. Some storms produced very heavy rainfall, primarily in Pima and Pinal Counties.  One storm even produced a brief funnel cloud near Tucson.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused washes and roads to flood on the southwest side of Tucson. A motorist was rescued near South Gila Avenue and Calle Torim. Three others were rescued from their Jeep where the Black Wash crosses Mardick Avenue.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very moist air of around 1.75 inches of precipitable water contributed to the development of thunderstorms across much of southeast Arizona. Some storms produced very heavy rainfall, primarily in Pima and Pinal Counties.  One storm even produced a brief funnel cloud near Tucson.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused several roads to be closed in the Mammoth area including Copper Creek Road and River Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms were nudged slightly eastward by low pressure to the west of Arizona. Storms produced damaging winds and heavy rain from Tucson eastward.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds knocked down 6 power poles along Wilmot Road south of Interstate 10 leaving 2500 customer without power.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough triggered evening thunderstorms over Cochise County that developed westward across southeast Arizona, resulting in pockets of damaging winds and heavy rain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds blew down a power pole near Bowie. About 2000 people lost electrical power.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough triggered evening thunderstorms over Cochise County that developed westward across southeast Arizona, resulting in pockets of damaging winds and heavy rain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding forced the closure of roads south of Sierra Vista as well as the Hereford and Tombstone areas. A man was rescued from his vehicle that was stuck in flood waters on Three Canyons Road west of Hereford.  Flood debris blocked part of Moson Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough triggered evening thunderstorms over Cochise County that developed westward across southeast Arizona, resulting in pockets of damaging winds and heavy rain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds blew apart a carport in Whetstone.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong push of monsoon moisture fueled a five-day outbreak of thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin. Numerous storms produced flash flooding and/or severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: County Road 1 was closed due to debris washing onto the road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms developed across portions of southwest Arizona during the afternoon hours on September 3rd. The most significant storms occurred over La Paz county. Due to the moist and unstable nature of the atmosphere, the storms produced locally heavy rainfall with peak rain rates in excess of 2 inches per hour. The excessive rain led to flash flooding which continued into the evening hours. Some areas affected included the towns of Bouse, Brenda and Quartzsite. Multiple Flash Flood Warnings were issued as a result of the flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms with locally heavy rainfall developed across portions of the southwest Arizona deserts, including far northern Yuma county, during the late afternoon hours on September 3rd. At about 5 pm, radar indicated that the storms had already produced 2 inches or more of rain near the Kofa National Wlldlife Refuge, including areas along State Route 95. At 608 pm, local law enforcement reported that State Route 95 was closed at milepost 90 due to flash flooding. The heavy rain and flooding prompted the issuance of a Flash Flood Warning at 509 pm, which ran through 815 pm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms developed across much of La Paz county during the afternoon and evening hours on July 14th. Due to the very moist and unstable nature of the atmosphere, many of the storms produced locally heavy rainfall which led to periods of flash flooding. Radar indicated that rainfall totals in excess of 2 inches occurred with some of the stronger storms, and the excessive rain caused washes to flow heavily, water to flow over roads and highways, and significant street flooding. Some communities affected included Bouse and Quartzsite. In addition, isolated thunderstorms generated wet microbursts, which produced gusty and damaging winds in the Quartzsite area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms developed across La Paz county during the late morning and afternoon hours on July 14th. Due to the very moist and unstable nature of the atmosphere, the many of the storms generated heavy rain which led to episodes of flash flooding. Thunderstorms developed in the area around Quartzsite late in the morning; as of 130 pm radar estimated that 2 to 3 inches of rain had fallen since 1130 am.  According to Arizona Department of Transportation, at 2 pm state highway 95 was closed at mile post 113.5 due to flooding. This location was in the town of Quartzsite.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms with heavy rain caused minor road damage to the less traveled roads with the Gladiator Fire area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused minor damage to some of the less traveled road in the Gladiator Fire area. One of the automated rain gauges around the fire reported 1.90 inches of rain.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A low pressure system interacted with deep monsoon moisture to produce widespread thunderstorms and flash flooding. A few storms also produced severe weather.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Water and debris flowed over Highway 95 in several places just north of Mohave Valley.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist southeast airflow increased precipitable water values to over 1.75 inches. Thunderstorms developed over much of southeast Arizona.  Storms produced heavy rain and flash flooding over Pima and Cochise Counties. Damaging winds occurred in Cochise County and strong outflows in Pima County created a dust storm that tracked northwest across Ironwood National Forest.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding closed streets in far southeast Tucson and in the Vail area including Old Spanish Trail, Collossal Cave Road, Escalante at Atterbury Wash and Harrison at the Pantano Wash.  Several cars were stranded in flood waters on Houghton Road between Bilby and Valencia Roads.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Several days of thunderstorms that produced heavy rain on the Tohono O'Odham Nation caused the Vamori Wash to fill and leave its banks resulting in the closure of a major road.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Daily rounds of heavy rain created high flows in Vamori Wash, causing it to leave its banks and flow across Indian Road 21, keeping the road inundated for several days. The peak flow occurred during the afternoon of September 11th.
